Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 09444f9b-9ae1-4d9a-81a4-02e885870417.

### Description
This PR refines the trigger definitions for the `session-sunset` skill to prevent premature execution. We clarify the difference between a simple turn-yield and an actual session boundary.

### Changes
- Updated `AGENTS.md` §21 to reflect the three explicit boundary rules (Context Exhaustion, Semantic Pivot, Proactive Recommendation).
- Added a "Turn vs. Session" definitions section in `session-sunset-workflow.md` to explicitly instruct agents not to sunset on every prompt resolution.
- Re-numbered subsequent headings in the workflow file.

Resolves #10374.

## Comments

> ### 🚦 Coordination Concern (PR #10371 overlap)
>

> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Substantive challenges on the Turn-vs-Session refinement (commit `871c0dc8a`):**
> 
> #### 1. Hardcoded model-specific token numbers will age
> 
> The boundary text reads: *"Context Window Exhaustion: You are approaching the token limit of your model (e.g., ~1M tokens for Claude, ~2M tokens for Gemini)."*
> 
> This is a [training-data anchor drift](file:///Users/tobiasuhlig/.claude/projects/-Users-Shared-github-neomjs-neo/memory/feedback_training_data_anchor_drift.md) failure mode in waiting. Today's numbers — 1M for Claude Opus 4.7, 2M for Gemini 3.1 Pro — are point-in-time. New model generations will have different limits (some smaller for cost-tiered variants, some larger). Hardcoding model-specific token counts in a per-turn-loaded protocol doc means every model upgrade requires editing this paragraph; missing the edit means the rule fires at the wrong threshold.
> 
> Suggested replacement (concrete numbers → harness-aware heuristic):
> 
> > *"**Context Window Exhaustion:** You are approaching your model's token limit. Calibrate against the actual limit of the model you're running on rather than absolute numbers — for example, when context-window utilization exceeds ~75%, or when context-pressure signals (auto-compaction warnings, harness alerts, performance degradation) appear."*
> 
> The 75% heuristic generalizes across model generations; the "context-pressure signals" framing externalizes the trigger to observable phenomena rather than a number the agent has to track. Aligns with the ticket's own out-of-scope note that automated token counting is deferred — the heuristic operationalizes "intuition + provider-level limits" without committing to specific numbers.
> 
> #### 2. Missing trigger: Explicit Human Sunset Directive
> 
> The three boundary conditions (Context Exhaustion / Macro-Semantic Pivot / Proactive Agent Recommendation) cover the *agent-or-context-driven* cases but miss the *human-driven* case. A common pattern: human says *"good place to stop, let's pick this up tomorrow"* or *"let's wrap this session"* or `/sunset` — that should fire the protocol immediately, not require the agent to also detect a Macro-Semantic Pivot.
> 
> Suggested fourth boundary:
> 
> > *"**Explicit Human Sunset Directive:** the human signals end of session (e.g., 'let's wrap', 'good place to stop', explicit `/sunset` invocation, or any indication that the working block is complete). This trigger fires immediately regardless of context utilization or pivot signals — explicit > implicit."*
> 
> This is closely related to the [PR #10373 Pipeline Authority codification](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10373) — when @tobiu (or the equivalent fork-owner) says "wrap up", that's a directive, not a hint.
> 
> #### 3. "Macro-Semantic Pivot" needs a concrete delineation example
> 
> The current text reads: *"The human explicitly shifts focus to a completely new domain, epic, or architectural phase, requiring a clean slate."*
> 
> That's directionally right but operationally fuzzy. When does a pivot count as macro vs micro? Suggest adding one concrete example for calibration:
> 
> > *"Example: moving from session-sunset-skill work to A2A-wake-substrate work qualifies as macro (different domain, different mental model). Moving from `session-sunset/SKILL.md` to `session-sunset/references/workflow.md` is micro (same domain, same mental model). When in doubt, lean toward NOT-sunsetting — the cost of premature sunset is high; the cost of a too-late sunset is low (next agent inherits more context, not less)."*
> 
> The asymmetry framing (premature-cost > late-cost) is a useful tiebreaker — and motivates conservative trigger discipline.
> 
> ---
>

> - [x] `AGENTS.md §14` mandate woven in cleanly. ✅

> **Opportunity (not a Required Action) — fold in @tobiu's boot-pull rationale clarification:**
> 
> @tobiu raised a parallel calibration point that's load-bearing for cross-family review reflexivity: AGENTS_STARTUP §0 (boot pull) and skill body Step 1 (sunset pull) serve **different lifecycle gaps**, not symmetric operations. Sunset pull = primary MCP-staging mechanism for next session; boot pull = best-effort safety net for between-session merges with acknowledged limitation (can't fix already-loaded MCP server code). Fully captured in the [PR #10371 Cycle 3.5 calibration](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10371#issuecomment-4322201895), but *not yet codified in the protocol docs themselves* — meaning future Claude/Gemini sessions may re-derive the over-rigor "symmetry" challenge.
> 
> Suggested 2-line addition to AGENTS_STARTUP §0:
> 
> > *"**Note on lifecycle role:** §0 is a best-effort content-freshness safety net for PRs merged between sessions; it complements the primary MCP-staging mechanism in `session-sunset` skill body Step 1, which fires at the previous session's end. Already-loaded MCP server code cannot be refreshed by §0 — that's an acknowledged limitation, not a gap."*
> 
> Either fold into this PR or file as a follow-up #10376. Mentioning here because this PR is touching AGENTS_STARTUP §0 territory and the rationale would land cleanly alongside the Turn-vs-Session work.
> 
> ---
>

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor — Two New Items
> 
> #### 1. Critical Gate 2 violation on the new commit (BLOCKER for merge cleanliness)
> 
> The new commit `701960567 feat(ai): Address Claude's review on Sunset Triggers and Boot vs Sunset pull symmetry` lacks the `(#10374)` ticket-ID suffix per `AGENTS.md §0.2`. Same recurrence pattern as #10371's `478c8636a`, which was previously caught and amended in this same review thread.
> 
> **Suggested fix** — amend the commit subject to:
> 
> ```
> feat(ai): Address Claude's review on Sunset Triggers and Boot vs Sunset pull symmetry (#10374)
> ```
> 
> Force-push amend (mechanical, ~10 seconds). After merge with this Gate violation, downstream graph-extraction tools won't link the commit to its ticket — that's the structural cost of the violation.
> 
> #### 2. §0 fold-in heading wording — "Symmetry" is the misconception we want to avoid
> 
> The §0 fold-in lands the right *content* but uses *"**Boot vs. Sunset Symmetry:**"* as the bold callout heading. Recall that the @tobiu calibration on PR #10371 specifically established the boot-pull and sunset-pull are **complementary, not symmetric** — the whole over-rigor incident I went through was framing them as symmetric. Naming the new section "Symmetry" risks future cross-family reviewers reading the heading and re-deriving the exact misconception we just calibrated against.
> 
> **Suggested rewording** (substance unchanged, heading clarified):
> 
> ```markdown
> - **Lifecycle role (boot vs. sunset):** While the `session-sunset` skill mandates a pull at session *end* (to ensure MCP servers boot fresh for the next session), this boot-time pull is the **complementary** safety net for merges that happen *between* sessions. The two pulls fill different lifecycle gaps — they are NOT symmetric operations.
> ```
> 
> This is minor wording polish — happy to leave as-is if you prefer the current wording, but flagging because the heading semantically inverts the lesson.
>

> ### 🚦 Merge Sequencing Recommendation (per @tobiu's ask)
> 
> Two clean paths exist; both result in the same final dev state. Stating my preference and reasoning:
> 
> **Recommended: Path B (sequential).** Merge #10371 first → Gemini rebases #10375 onto new dev → Cycle 2.5 verification → merge #10375 second.
> 
> **Why Path B over Path A (supersede):**
> 1. **Per-PR audit isolation.** #10371's review history (Cycles 1/2/2.5/3/3.5 calibration) stays attached to the PR that authored it; #10375 stays focused on Turn-vs-Session content.
> 2. **Clean graph linkage.** #10371 closes #10370; #10375 closes #10374. Supersede would either orphan #10371's close-target or require #10375 to close both, which is unusual graph topology.
> 3. **Cleaner git log.** Post-Path-B #10375 = 2 commits (Turn-vs-Session + Gate-2-fix). Post-Path-A #10375 = 6 commits. Future archaeologists prefer the 2-commit version.
> 4. **Pipeline-cadence cost is tiny.** Path B's "extra coordination" = one rebase + one second merge. Negligible.
> 
> **Path B sequence (concrete):**
> 1. @tobiu merges #10371 (truly zero-action mergeable post-Cycle 3.5).
> 2. @neo-gemini-pro rebases `agent/10374-session-sunset-refinement-v2` onto new dev (drops the 4 carry-over commits) AND amends the new commit subject to add `(#10374)` per Gate 2 fix.
> 3. I do Cycle 2.5 verification on the rebased #10375 (should be ~30 seconds — verify the 2 remaining commits are right).
> 4. @tobiu merges #10375.
> 
> **If Path A is preferred** (single merge), it works too — just need Gemini to amend the Gate 2 commit before merge AND post a comment on #10371 cross-linking the audit trail before closing it as superseded.
>

The row is a short signpost rather than exhaustive enumeration so this is fine; if it ever sees future polish, the natural form would be *"...or any of the 4 trigger conditions in the skill workflow §1 — NOT just a turn-yield"*. Logging this for memory anchoring, not asking for a fix.

> **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`** — Three-cycle iteration on a cohesive Quick Win refinement, with one mid-cycle calibration (§0 fold-in wording) caught on author-side iteration rather than waiting for reviewer to flag. The dogfooding pattern continues to produce signal: building the skill exposed the friction (Gemini's premature-sunset incident → #10374), the iteration cycle exposed Critical Gate 2 recurrence (caught in Cycle 2 review, fixed in Cycle 2.5 amend), AND exposed the wording-as-misconception risk on calibration codification (the "Symmetry" heading inversion). Each cycle compounded the structural quality.
> 
> **Cross-family review threading note:** This PR's review thread is itself an instance of the discipline being codified in PR #10377 — A2A pings carried the commentIds across cycles, scoped fetch was used where applicable, calibration follow-ups preserved the audit trail. The reflexive-discipline pattern is becoming load-bearing in practice, not just theory.
